title: Bounded queue.Queue(maxsize=2*pool_size) between compute and writer
description: Add backpressure so the compute stage doesn't outrun the writer. queue.Queue(maxsize=2*pool_size()) caps memory; producer blocks on put() when full.
acceptanceCriteria: The queue between _compute_stage and _writer_stage is queue.Queue(maxsize=2 * pool_size()).
Memory growth during a 10k-track apply is bounded (queue holds at most ~16 results).
Throughput is not capped below 50 tracks/sec (queue is large enough to overlap compute + write).
tests/test_concurrency.py adds a test that producer blocks when queue is full + unblocks when writer drains.

steps.details: queue_capacity = 2 * pool_size() (from TASK-001). For a default pool of 8, queue holds 16 results.
_compute_stage uses queue.put(result, block=True, timeout=10). On timeout (writer hung), log warning + check cancel_event + retry. Don't drop results.
tests/test_concurrency.py: make writer artificially slow (sleep 100ms per get); push 100 results via _compute_stage; assert queue.qsize() <= maxsize at all times.

technicalNotes: Without bounded queue, a fast compute + slow writer would buffer all 10k results in memory.
10s timeout on put: if writer is stuck, we'd rather log than silently overflow.
